hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 5, Q. Your opponent called your raise before the cards were dealt.
River — 4. Your opponent bet large — about the size of the pot.

wrongReasons: 10 J has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with 10 J.
2 K makes a full house here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
6 8 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only one pair wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: Q 6

Your opponent was drawing to a flush and called the flop to keep their options open. Once they hit, they bet the turn and river for value — growing aggression on later streets is a hallmark of a completed draw.
